# DeathPush

> A Git GUI for terminal power users. Review diffs, stage changes, and manage branches without opening an IDE. Built for Claude Code, Codex, and Vim workflows.

## About

DeathPush is an open-source Git GUI built with Swift and Rust for macOS. It gives terminal-first developers (Claude Code, Codex, Vim) a fast way to review diffs, stage changes, and manage branches without loading a full IDE.

## Key Features

- Visual staging with inline diff toggles, file-level and chunk-level control
- Monaco-powered diffs with syntax highlighting, inline editing, side-by-side and unified views
- Branch management: create, switch, merge, rebase, rename, delete, remote tracking
- Image diffs with side-by-side, overlay, and difference modes
- Git blame with line-by-line annotations and file history timeline
- Integrated terminal with PTY support
- Stash management, tag creation, cherry-pick support
- Multi-window support with independent workspaces
- Multiple built-in themes (dark, light, high-contrast)
- Native performance via Swift + Rust backend

## Technical Details

- Stack: Swift (UI), Rust (core)
- License: Apache-2.0
- Platforms: macOS (Apple Silicon & Intel)
- Version: 0.1.0

## Links

- Website: https://deathpush.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/felipefdl/deathpush
